disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A firm lump in the right breast present for about six months, initially painless and not acted upon
trigger_question_examples: How long has the lump been there?
When did you first notice something in the breast?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I found it about six months ago. It was hard and it didn't hurt, so I convinced myself it was nothing and left it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lump feels hard and does not move freely under the fingers
trigger_question_examples: Does the lump move when you press it, or does it feel fixed?
How does the lump feel to you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's hard, and honestly it doesn't really move — it feels stuck in place when I press around it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New inward pulling of the nipple and a change in the breast contour over the last month
trigger_question_examples: Has the nipple or the shape of the breast changed?
Does the breast look different from the other side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Over the last month the nipple has pulled inward and the breast has changed shape — it doesn't look like the other one anymore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dimpled, puckered orange-peel appearance of the skin over the lump, developed over the last 48 hours
trigger_question_examples: Has the skin over the breast changed — any dimpling, thickening or an orange-peel look?
Can you describe what the skin over the lump looks like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: In the last day or two the skin over the lump has gone dimpled and puckered, a bit like the skin of an orange.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Spontaneous unilateral bloody and serous nipple discharge that started this evening
trigger_question_examples: Is there any discharge from the nipple, and what colour is it?
You mentioned bleeding — where is it coming from?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: This evening it started leaking on its own — clear fluid and some blood coming from the nipple. I didn't squeeze it, it just started.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe, continuous, burning breast pain not relieved by over-the-counter paracetamol
trigger_question_examples: How bad is the pain and what does it feel like?
Has anything helped the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a severe, constant burning pain, the worst it's been, and the paracetamol I've been taking does nothing for it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: One-month history of generalized severe fatigue and unintentional weight loss
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any weight loss, or been more tired than usual?
How has your energy and appetite been over the last month?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you ask — yes, I've been extremely tired for about a month and I've lost weight without trying.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No high fever, no shaking chills or rigors, and no obvious fluctuant swelling or hot red spreading area
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, sweats or shaking chills?
Is there a hot, red, spreading area or a soft swelling that feels like it's full of fluid?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or chills — my husband checked and the thermometer said 37.4. There's no hot red patch or soft fluid-filled lump that I can feel, it's more the hard lump and the skin change.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new persistent localized bone pain at present
trigger_question_examples: Any new deep or persistent bone pain, in the back, hips or elsewhere?
Have you had any new aches in your bones that don't settle?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't had any new bone pain or aches like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Has been avoiding medical assessment for months because of fear, with self-isolation and high risk of non-engagement with follow-up
trigger_question_examples: It sounds like this has been weighing on you — what has stopped you getting it looked at?
How have you been coping with this over the last few months?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been too frightened to have it checked, so I kept to myself and put it off. I'm worried I won't be able to make myself go through with the tests.
